planets.name: Think
planets.summary: Archive cluster around think: 461 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2026-01-26
artifacts.excerpt: @hermittoday i think we do pick up trauma and other somatic residue much easier even with the same stimuli
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, things, thing.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: I've been developing a framework that unifies a bunch of seemingly unrelated things: placebo effect, gratitude practice, meditation on pleasant sensations, nervous system retraining, tinnitus habituation. I think they're all special cases of one core principle.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, things, thing.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: Even just thinking about doing some things felt worse than pain. I'd rather have been punched in the gut than think about doing dishes or getting into cold water. Every potential action carried this aversive charge. I call this threat-valence.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, things, thing.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: This is why the same intervention doesn't work for everyone. We're all running different models built from different histories of attention and meaning-making. Let me walk through the phenomena I think are special cases of this principle.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, things, thing.

planets.name: Don
planets.summary: Archive cluster around don: 373 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2026-06-23
artifacts.excerpt: A situation that's bothering you, even something as small as an awkward interaction with a cashier? Conjure the feeling, put it into your hand, and write it down. Doesn't have to be pretty, or even real words or letters. The studies show that typing or dictation don't work.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, things.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: Don't force things. Gentler methods tend to be safer than forceful ones. The trifecta of increased safety, enjoyment, and activation seems particularly powerful for me personally. Trying to do just one is often counterproductive. Two, maybe, but listen to your gut.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, things.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2025-11-28
artifacts.excerpt: @nosilverv America is rich compared to Japan, but they design their spaces (and products) to be a joy to be in. To respect dignity. The seatbelt plays a welcome melody until you fasten it, not beeps at you until you shut it up. I don't know what the lesson is here.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, things.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2020-07-11
artifacts.excerpt: @representus You don't entirely avoid the spoiler effect with Ranked Choice. You do with #ApprovalVoting or #ScoreVoting, and both are simpler because you don't have to change your marks for the other candidates when you re-evaluate one of them. Still far better than plurality voting.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, things.

planets.name: Feel
planets.summary: Archive cluster around feel: 282 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2026-04-29
artifacts.excerpt: Have you heard of nociplasticity? It's when brain hardware that normally processes other stuff (like emotions) starts processing pain and suffering instead. So you feel more suck, less the other stuff. Unlearning this has been a huge benefit of meditative/somatic work for me.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, better, things, bad.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2025-05-20
artifacts.excerpt: @maiamindel If you're healthy, yes. There was a long while that I made myself feel much worse trying to exercise because my nervous system was fucked up
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, better, things, bad.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: The vicious cycle: lack of safety/pleasure → nervous system biases negative → same sensations feel stronger and worse → thoughts become negative → try things that used to help relax → they suck now → stress about declining health → internal conflict → more lack of safety.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, better, things, bad.

artifacts.title: Feel receipt from 2026-04-08
artifacts.excerpt: I recently taught a class called How To Feel Better with the San Diego @fractal_nyc affiliate, Praxis Community U. It's a distillation of what I've learned and tried over 5 years. I recently uploaded everything to my site so now you have access to it all. https://t.co/JgXV1mkusx
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: feel, better, things, bad.

planets.name: Time
planets.summary: Archive cluster around time: 293 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: I used to experience essentially no positive sensation. Beauty was gone, pleasure was gone, even wanting things was gone. Now I genuinely enjoy being alive most of the time. The path out connected placebo, meditation, tinnitus treatment, and what I'm calling the shaman cluster.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: time, first, long, work.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: Meditation on pleasant sensations (if there are any) is maybe the most direct application. You attend to warmth, relaxation, subtle pleasure. The system learns: "when I attend to the body, I find pleasantness." Over time, this shifts the default.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: time, first, long, work.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2026-06-23
artifacts.excerpt: Spending some time with family after vibecamp, I've been talking with one family member with debilitating nociplastic pain. Her doctor, a psychiatrist with a focus on Sarno TMS, has essentially given her prescription strength witchcraft, and it's working. Here's how to do it.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: time, first, long, work.

artifacts.title: Time receipt from 2025-12-30
artifacts.excerpt: In Buddhist dependent origination, feeling-tone arises from contact and conditions craving. The insight: this link isn't fixed. You can interrupt the cascade from sensation to reaction, and over time, reshape the arising of feeling-tone itself.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: time, first, long, work.
